summary: OpenAI introduced a new $100/month ChatGPT Pro tier, creating a mid-market price point between Plus at $20 and the existing $200 plan while offering 5x more Codex usage for heavier coding workflows. ByteDance Seed simultaneously surfaced multiple model artifacts — Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview and Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM — signaling continued competition in multimodal and speech-native systems. On the research side, the DMax paper proposed a new decoding method for diffusion language models aimed at much higher parallelism without the usual quality collapse, a potentially important efficiency lever if results hold up.

The company also introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, emphasizing attentive listening, interference suppression, and more natural turn-taking in voice interaction.
The cluster points to simultaneous investment in text/model quality and real-time speech systems, a combination increasingly important for assistants, customer support, and device-native AI.

top_topics.HEADLINE: OpenAI Launches ChatGPT Pro at $100/Month With 5x More Codex Usage
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: The new ChatGPT Pro plan is priced at $100 per month, filling the gap between OpenAI's $20 Plus tier and its $200 higher-end offering.
OpenAI says the $100 tier includes 5x more usage of Codex than ChatGPT Plus, positioning it for longer and more intensive coding sessions.
The move suggests OpenAI sees enough demand from prosumers and developers to support a materially higher ARPU tier without forcing users directly to $200 pricing.

top_topics.title: DMax: Aggressive Parallel Decoding for dLLMs
top_topics.summary: This paper introduces DMax, a decoding method for diffusion language models. It aims to reduce error accumulation during parallel decoding so dLLMs can decode more aggressively without large quality losses.

items.title: DMax: Aggressive Parallel Decoding for dLLMs
items.summary: We present DMax, a new paradigm for efficient diffusion language models (dLLMs). It mitigates error accumulation in parallel decoding, enabling aggressive decoding parallelism while preserving generation quality. Unlike conventional masked dLLMs that decode through a binary mask-to-token transition, DMax reformulates decoding as a progressive self-refinement from mask embeddings to token embeddings. At the core of our approach is On-Policy Uniform Training, a novel training strategy that efficiently unifies masked and uniform dLLMs, equipping the model to recover clean tokens from both mask...

metadata.ai_summary: DMax introduces a novel approach for efficient diffusion language models that reduces error accumulation during parallel decoding through self-refinement and unified training strategies.
metadata.spotlight_comment: We present DMax, a new paradigm for efficient diffusion language models (dLLMs). It mitigates error accumulation in parallel decoding, enabling aggressive decoding parallelism while preserving generation quality. Unlike conventional masked dLLMs that decode through a binary mask-to-token transiti...

items.title: DMax: Aggressive Parallel Decoding for dLLMs
items.summary: We present DMax, a new paradigm for efficient diffusion language models (dLLMs). It mitigates error accumulation in parallel decoding, enabling aggressive decoding parallelism while preserving generation quality. Unlike conventional masked dLLMs that decode through a binary mask-to-token transition, DMax reformulates decoding as a progressive self-refinement from mask embeddings to token embeddings. At the core of our approach is On-Policy Uniform Training, a novel training strategy that efficiently unifies masked and uniform dLLMs, equipping the model to recover clean tokens from both mask...

metadata.comment: Reformulates diffusion-LM decoding as progressive self-refinement and trains on-policy recovery from its own prediction errors for aggressive parallel decoding.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: This paper introduces DMax, a decoding method for diffusion language models. It aims to reduce error accumulation during parallel decoding so dLLMs can decode more aggressively without large quality losses.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: If replicated, DMax could improve inference efficiency for diffusion language models and reopen the case for dLLMs as a practical alternative to autoregressive systems.
top_topics.HEADLINE: DMax Paper Proposes Aggressive Parallel Decoding for Diffusion Language Models
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: The paper introduces DMax as a decoding paradigm designed to reduce error accumulation during parallel generation in diffusion language models.
According to the authors, DMax enables more aggressive decoding parallelism while preserving generation quality, targeting one of the core bottlenecks in dLLM deployment.
The work was surfaced via both Hugging Face Trending Papers and arXiv tracking, but the current evidence is still paper-level rather than production validation.

topics.title: Meta's new model is Muse Spark, and meta.ai chat has some interesting tools
topics.summary: This post discusses Meta's release of Muse Spark and highlights tools available in the meta.ai chat product. It is an editorial analysis of Meta's latest model and interface features.

items.title: Meta's new model is Muse Spark, and meta.ai chat has some interesting tools
items.summary:
Meta announced Muse Spark today, their first model release since Llama 4 almost exactly a year ago. It's hosted, not open weights, and the API is currently "a private API preview to select users", but you can try it out today on meta.ai (Facebook or Instagram login required).

Meta's self-reported benchmarks show it competitive with Opus 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and GPT 5.4 on selected benchmarks, though notably behind on Terminal-Bench 2.0. Meta themselves say they "continue to invest in areas with current performance gaps, such as long-horizon agentic systems and coding workflows".

The model is exposed as two di...

topics.title: Introducing stateful MCP client capabilities on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime
topics.summary: AWS published a technical post on adding stateful MCP client capabilities to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime. The post explains how to build stateful MCP servers that can request user input, invoke LLM sampling, and stream progress updates.

items.title: Introducing stateful MCP client capabilities on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime
items.summary: In this post, you will learn how to build stateful MCP servers that request user input during execution, invoke LLM sampling for dynamic content generation, and stream progress updates for long-running tasks. You will see code examples for each capability and deploy a working stateful MCP server to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime.

items.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
items.summary: This post walks you through three steps: starting a session and generating the Live View URL, rendering the stream in your React application, and wiring up an AI agent that drives the browser while your users watch. At the end, you will have a working sample application you can clone and run.

items.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
items.summary: This post shows you how to manage FM transitions in Amazon Bedrock, so you can make sure your AI applications remain operational as models evolve. We discuss the three lifecycle states, how to plan migrations with the new extended access feature, and practical strategies to transition your applications to newer models without disruption.

topics.SHORT_COMMENT: AWS published a technical post on adding stateful MCP client capabilities to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime. The post explains how to build stateful MCP servers that can request user input, invoke LLM sampling, and stream progress updates.

items.summary:
Tool: GitHub Repo Size

GitHub doesn't tell you the repo size in the UI, but it's available in the CORS-friendly API. Paste a repo into this tool to see the size, for example for simonw/datasette (8.1MB).

Tags: cors, github

topics.SHORT_COMMENT: Simon Willison published a tool called GitHub Repo Size. It exposes repository size information from GitHub's API that is not shown directly in the GitHub web interface.
topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: This tooling surfaced across 1 independent source(s).
topics.HEADLINE: GitHub Repo Size

topics.title: Spectral Edge Dynamics Reveal Functional Modes of Learning
topics.summary: This paper analyzes the spectral edge of parameter updates to identify low-dimensional functional modes of learning. It applies the method to grokking and argues that these modes are not captured by standard interpretability tools.

items.title: Spectral Edge Dynamics Reveal Functional Modes of Learning
items.summary: Broadly important today: Uses spectral-edge analysis of parameter updates to reveal low-dimensional functional modes of learning during grokking that standard interpretability tools miss.

metadata.comment: Uses spectral-edge analysis of parameter updates to reveal low-dimensional functional modes of learning during grokking that standard interpretability tools miss.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Uses spectral-edge analysis of parameter updates to reveal low-dimensional functional modes of learning during grokking that standard interpretability tools miss.

items.title: The Lifecycle of the Spectral Edge: From Gradient Learning to Weight-Decay Compression
items.summary: Broadly important today: Dissects the spectral edge during grokking into gradient and weight-decay components, showing a transition from functional direction to compression axis.

metadata.comment: Dissects the spectral edge during grokking into gradient and weight-decay components, showing a transition from functional direction to compression axis.

topics.title: Sensitivity-Positional Co-Localization in GQA Transformers
topics.summary: We investigate a fundamental structural question in Grouped Query Attention (GQA) transformers: do the layers most sensitive to task correctness coincide with the layers where positional encoding adaptation has the greatest leverage? We term this the co-localization hypothesis and test it on Llama 3.1 8B, a 32-layer GQA model with a 4:1 query-to-key-value head ratio. We introduce \LSLORA, which restricts LoRA adaptation to layers identified via a novel correctness-differential hidden-state metric, and GARFA (GQA-Aware RoPE Frequency Adaptation), which attaches 8 learnable per-KV-head scalar...

metadata.comment: Finds strong anti-localization between task-sensitive layers and RoPE-sensitive layers in GQA transformers, then exploits that structure for targeted adaptation.

topics.title: Dead Weights, Live Signals: Feedforward Graphs of Frozen Language Models
topics.summary: We present a feedforward graph architecture in which heterogeneous frozen large language models serve as computational nodes, communicating through a shared continuous latent space via learned linear projections. Building on recent work demonstrating geometric compatibility between independently trained LLM latent spaces~\cite{armstrong2026thinking}, we extend this finding from static two-model steering to end-to-end trainable multi-node graphs, where projection matrices are optimized jointly via backpropagation through residual stream injection hooks. Three small frozen models (Llama-3.2-1...

topics.title: Exponential quantum advantage in processing massive classical data
topics.summary: This paper claims exponential quantum advantage for processing large-scale classical data. It presents explicit theoretical results supporting that advantage.

paper_spotlight.label: Daily Hot Papers
paper_spotlight.description: Papers that feel broadly important to the day and belong in the hotspot paper feed.

items.title: SkillClaw: Let Skills Evolve Collectively with Agentic Evolver
items.summary: Large language model (LLM) agents such as OpenClaw rely on reusable skills to perform complex tasks, yet these skills remain largely static after deployment. As a result, similar workflows, tool usage patterns, and failure modes are repeatedly rediscovered across users, preventing the system from improving with experience. While interactions from different users provide complementary signals about when a skill works or fails, existing systems lack a mechanism to convert such heterogeneous experiences into reliable skill updates. To address these issues, we present SkillClaw, a framework for...
items.spotlight_comment: Large language model (LLM) agents such as OpenClaw rely on reusable skills to perform complex tasks, yet these skills remain largely static after deployment. As a result, similar workflows, tool usage patterns, and failure modes are repeatedly rediscovered across users, preventing the system from...

items.title: When Numbers Speak: Aligning Textual Numerals and Visual Instances in Text-to-Video Diffusion Models
items.summary: Text-to-video diffusion models have enabled open-ended video synthesis, but often struggle with generating the correct number of objects specified in a prompt. We introduce NUMINA , a training-free identify-then-guide framework for improved numerical alignment. NUMINA identifies prompt-layout inconsistencies by selecting discriminative self- and cross-attention heads to derive a countable latent layout. It then refines this layout conservatively and modulates cross-attention to guide regeneration. On the introduced CountBench, NUMINA improves counting accuracy by up to 7.4% on Wan2.1-1.3B,...
items.spotlight_comment: Text-to-video diffusion models have enabled open-ended video synthesis, but often struggle with generating the correct number of objects specified in a prompt. We introduce NUMINA , a training-free identify-then-guide framework for improved numerical alignment. NUMINA identifies prompt-layout inc...

items.title: MegaStyle: Constructing Diverse and Scalable Style Dataset via Consistent Text-to-Image Style Mapping
items.summary: In this paper, we introduce MegaStyle, a novel and scalable data curation pipeline that constructs an intra-style consistent, inter-style diverse and high-quality style dataset. We achieve this by leveraging the consistent text-to-image style mapping capability of current large generative models, which can generate images in the same style from a given style description. Building on this foundation, we curate a diverse and balanced prompt gallery with 170K style prompts and 400K content prompts, and generate a large-scale style dataset MegaStyle-1.4M via content-style prompt combinations. W...
items.spotlight_comment: In this paper, we introduce MegaStyle, a novel and scalable data curation pipeline that constructs an intra-style consistent, inter-style diverse and high-quality style dataset. We achieve this by leveraging the consistent text-to-image style mapping capability of current large generative models,...

items.title: KnowU-Bench: Towards Interactive, Proactive, and Personalized Mobile Agent Evaluation
items.summary: Personalized mobile agents that infer user preferences and calibrate proactive assistance hold great promise as everyday digital assistants, yet existing benchmarks fail to capture what this requires. Prior work evaluates preference recovery from static histories or intent prediction from fixed contexts. Neither tests whether an agent can elicit missing preferences through interaction, nor whether it can decide when to intervene, seek consent, or remain silent in a live GUI environment. We introduce KnowU-Bench, an online benchmark for personalized mobile agents built on a reproducible Andr...
items.spotlight_comment: Personalized mobile agents that infer user preferences and calibrate proactive assistance hold great promise as everyday digital assistants, yet existing benchmarks fail to capture what this requires. Prior work evaluates preference recovery from static histories or intent prediction from fixed c...

items.title: Externalization in LLM Agents: A Unified Review of Memory, Skills, Protocols and Harness Engineering
items.summary: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly built less by changing model weights than by reorganizing the runtime around them. Capabilities that earlier systems expected the model to recover internally are now externalized into memory stores, reusable skills, interaction protocols, and the surrounding harness that makes these modules reliable in practice. This paper reviews that shift through the lens of externalization. Drawing on the idea of cognitive artifacts, we argue that agent infrastructure matters not merely because it adds auxiliary components, but because it transforms har...
items.spotlight_comment: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly built less by changing model weights than by reorganizing the runtime around them. items.title: Optimal Decay Spectra for Linear Recurrences
items.summary: Broadly important today: Derives minimax-optimal decay spectrum design for linear recurrent models and proposes position-adaptive spectral tapering across Mamba/RWKV-like architectures.

x_buzz.title: ChatGPT has a new $100 per month Pro subscription
x_buzz.summary: OpenAI has announced a new version of its ChatGPT Pro subscription that costs $100 per month. The new Pro tier offers "5x more" usage of its Codex coding tool than the $20 per month Plus subscription and "is best for longer, high-effort Codex sessions," OpenAI says. The company is introducing the new tier as it […]

x_buzz.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
x_buzz.summary: Anthropic said this week that it limited the release of its newest model, dubbed Mythos, because it is too capable of finding security exploits in software relied upon by users around the world. Are real cybersecurity concerns a cover for a bigger problem at the frontier lab?
